title: Abetment of a thing
content: A person abets the doing of a thing, who: First.—Instigates any person to do that thing; or Secondly.—Engages in any conspiracy for the doing of that thing...; or Thirdly.—Intentionally aids, by any act or illegal omission, the doing of that thing.

illumination_lamp.scenario: A instigates B to murder Z. B refuses.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: A is guilty of abetting murder.

footnotes.text: An omission is only illegal if it violates a legal duty to act.

title: Abettor
content: A person abets an offence, who abets either the commission of an offence, or the commission of an act which would be an offence, if committed by a person capable by law of committing an offence...

footnotes.text: Abettor is guilty even if the principal is legally incapable (e.g., child or unsound mind).

title: Punishment of abetment if the act abetted is committed in consequence
content: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment... be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.

footnotes.text: If the act is committed, the abettor is punished as if they committed it themselves.

title: Punishment of abetment if person abetted does act with different intention
content: Whoever abets... shall, if the person abetted does the act with a different intention or knowledge... be punished with the punishment provided for the offence which would have been committed if the act had been done with the intention of the abettor.

title: Liability of abettor when one act abetted and different act done
content: When an act is abetted and a different act is done, the abettor is liable for the act done... provided the act done was a probable consequence of the abetment.

title: Abettor when liable to cumulative punishment for act abetted and for act done
content: If the act for which the abettor is liable under the last preceding section is committed in addition to the act abetted, and constitutes a distinct offence, the abettor is liable to punishment for each of the offences.

title: Liability of abettor for an effect caused by the act abetted different from that intended
content: When an act is abetted with the intention of causing a particular effect, and an act... causes a different effect... the abettor is liable for the effect caused, provided he knew that the act abetted was likely to cause that effect.

title: Abettor present, when offence is committed
content: Whenever any person, who if absent would be liable to be punished as an abettor, is present when the act or offence... is committed, he shall be deemed to have committed such act or offence.

illumination_lamp.scenario: A instigates B to hit Z; A is present.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: A is deemed to have committed the assault.

title: Abetment of offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life
content: Whoever abets... punishable with death or imprisonment for life, shall, if that offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment... be punished with imprisonment up to seven years, and fine.

title: Abetment of offence punishable with imprisonment
content: Whoever abets an offence punishable with imprisonment shall, if that offence be not committed... be punished with imprisonment of any description provided for that offence, for a term which may extend to one-fourth part of the longest term provided for that offence.

title: Abetting commission of offence by the public or by more than ten persons
content: Whoever abets the commission of an offence by the public generally or by any number or class of persons exceeding ten, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or both.

title: Concealing design to commit offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life
content: Whoever, intending to facilitate... the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, voluntarily conceals... shall, if that offence be committed, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years.

title: Public servant concealing design to commit offence which it is his duty to prevent
content: Whoever, being a public servant, intending to facilitate... the commission of an offence which it is his duty to prevent... voluntarily conceals... shall be punished with imprisonment extending to half of the longest term for the offence.

title: Concealing design to commit offence punishable with imprisonment
content: Whoever, intending to facilitate... commission of an offence punishable with imprisonment, voluntarily conceals... shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one-fourth of the longest term provided for that offence.
